surface.name: Chronosphere Observability Platform role-based getting-started routes
surface.selection_basis: The official general getting-started page first requires organization-specific authentication, then presents different administrator and developer task families. It does not name one universal first task or terminal, and several routes require telemetry already flowing or resources already created by an administrator.

alternatives_considered.name: Administrator telemetry ingestion
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires selecting metrics, traces, or logs and an organization-specific ingestion route.

alternatives_considered.name: Developer telemetry query
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This assumes a tenant with telemetry and is one of several developer routes.

alternatives_considered.name: Dashboard creation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This assumes access and data, and the general guide does not promote it over alerts, services, queries, or integration work.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without assuming a Chronosphere tenant, user account, role, team, permissions, telemetry, dashboard, monitor, or saved resource, and identify the platform-wide first documented value outcome.
assumed_prior_state: No organization tenant URL, administrator invitation, account, SSO identity, personal access token, team membership, telemetry, dashboard, alert, service, notebook, favorite, or pinned scope is assumed.
A supported browser, email access, and network connection are available.
No invitation was requested or accepted, authentication was completed, telemetry was ingested or queried, or resource was created during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: No single platform-wide first success is established by the general Observability Platform documentation.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A human must choose an administrator or developer objective and provide an organization tenant with the required access and telemetry context before a first-success route can be selected.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: No universal signal is named. Possible results include successful access, flowing telemetry, a dashboard, an alert, a service view, a query result, an integration, a favorite, or a pinned scope.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The general guide lists shared navigation actions, then splits into administrator tasks for access, ingestion, monitoring, and telemetry control, and developer tasks for querying, observing, integrating, and configuring resources.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The choice changes the actor, prerequisites, data dependencies, steps, and terminal. Selecting one without a stated role and outcome would substitute researcher judgment for the official general documentation.

prerequisites.requirement: Have access to an organization-specific Chronosphere Observability Platform tenant URL.
Have an administrator-provisioned user account or organization-enabled SSO identity.
Have the role and team permissions required for the selected administrator or developer task.
Have metrics, traces, logs, or change-event data available when the selected outcome depends on observing or querying telemetry.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Observability Platform and review the common, administrator, and developer routes.
details: Common actions include authentication, home navigation, dashboards, monitors, queries, and service exploration.
Administrator work covers access, ingestion, monitoring, and control; developer work covers queries, observation, integrations, and resource configuration.
primary_path.output: The role-based task families and their dependencies are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The official page makes the required role and objective choice explicit.

primary_path.action: Stop before selecting a task or attempting authentication because no universal tenant, role, data context, or first-success outcome is established.
details: Interactive authentication is configured per organization as SSO, email and password, or both.
Email-and-password users must be invited by an administrator; SSO users depend on the organization's identity provider.
primary_path.input: The unresolved organization, role, access, telemetry, and outcome context
primary_path.output: No route is selected and no external state changes.
primary_path.success_signal: The record preserves the official decision boundary.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A human must choose the intended task and provide the organization access and data context.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The official guide requires a choice among controlling access, ingesting metrics, traces, or logs, creating dashboards, alerts, or services after data flows, controlling telemetry, querying data, observing existing telemetry, integrating command-line tools or APIs, or configuring favorites and pinned scopes. Access also depends on an organization tenant and either an administrator invitation with signup, email verification, and password sign-in, or an organization-configured SSO redirect. The docs do not choose one of these routes as the platform-wide first success.
candidate_path_gap.impact: There is no single chronological path, input set, or completion signal to encode. A human must define the role, tenant, telemetry state, and desired value outcome before one route can be researched end to end.

branches.path: Open the tenant login URL, click Log in with Single Sign On, authenticate with the external provider, and return to the Observability Platform.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces an external identity-provider boundary before the chosen task.

branches.condition: The organization uses email and password
branches.path: Receive an administrator invitation, open its link, enter account details including a password, click Sign Up, open the verification email, click its link, then sign in at the tenant login URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds administrator provisioning, email verification, and password authentication before the chosen task.

branches.path: Choose access control, telemetry ingestion, dashboards and alerts, services, or telemetry-control work.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the first result to an administrative, ingestion, monitoring, or cost-control outcome.

branches.path: Choose query, investigation, dashboard or Lens observation, tool integration, favorite, or pinned-scope work.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the first result to a query, visualization, integration, or personal-configuration outcome.

friction_gates.description: The general guide requires a role and task choice before a specific route can be followed.
The user needs an organization-specific tenant URL and account access.
Email-and-password accounts are requested by an organization administrator.
The email-and-password route requires opening a verification email link after signup.
Dashboard, alert, service, query, and investigation outcomes depend on telemetry already being ingested.
Teams and roles determine access to platform resources and administrative capabilities.

sources.title: Get started with Observability Platform - Chronosphere Documentation
Authenticate with Observability Platform as a user - Chronosphere Documentation
sections_used: Authenticate with a personal access token

sources.title: User accounts - Chronosphere Documentation
sections_used: Grant System Administrator (SysAdmin) privileges to users

uncertainties.question: Which administrator or developer outcome should define Chronosphere first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes the actor, prerequisites, telemetry dependency, steps, and terminal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official general page presents multiple task families without selecting one universal route.

uncertainties.question: What tenant, authentication method, permissions, and telemetry would the developer have?
uncertainties.impact: These determine whether the user can enter the platform and which tasks are possible.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Chronosphere documents organization-specific access and role-dependent resources, but no general visitor has that context.

excluded_after_success: No after-success scope is excluded because a platform-wide first-success outcome has not been selected.
